---
title: "Can't change to 96x96 nvidia driver"
date: 2008-02-25
forum: General Help
---

### Post by tirux on 2008-02-25
I am trying to find a way to change my DPI in my xorg.conf file to 96x96 but I am pretty stuck at 89x87.
I am using the latest nvidia driver for GeForce 7900GS with a resolution of 1440x900. If I disable this driver, I can get back at 96x96 without a problem, but I need this driver in order to watch videos from my TV-Out video card.

Can someone please check my xorg file and see what could be wrong? :confused:


# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings

### Post by guano on 2008-02-26
Hi there

[http://linux-blog.org/index.php?/archives/227-KDE-and-Xorg,-Fonts-and-DPI.html](http://linux-blog.org/index.php?/archives/227-KDE-and-Xorg,-Fonts-and-DPI.html)

### Post by x1a4 on 2008-02-26
Hi,

In your xorg.conf, set 'DisplaySize' according to the formula in guano's link, then, in section "Screen" add the following option *above* the DPI setting
```
Option         "UseEdidDpi" "false"
```

Correctly setting modelines can also help.  Use **gtf** to get the correct modelines for all your resolutions and refresh rates, and add them to your xorg.conf's "Monitor" section.

Restart the X server once you've made your changes
```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart
```

---
